raw_observation: Blender 5.0.1 evaluated four ordinary, contaminated, parallel, and oblique vector pairs; every unit-length, pairwise-dot, and handedness error remained at or below 8.274946261543903e-08.
candidate_causes: Projection removes normal contamination from the supplied tangent.
The least-aligned-axis fallback avoids a zero vector for parallel inputs.
Final cross-product reconstruction restores a right-handed orthonormal basis.
interpretation: The proposed vector construction is numerically suitable for a future Blender 5.0.1 node builder, within the tested cases and tolerance.
discriminating_tests: Build the equivalent shader or Geometry Nodes graph and compare exported diagnostic vectors.
Test real imported tangent attributes, mirrored UV islands, negative transforms, and degenerate mesh regions.
Render anisotropic response while rotating the panel and tangent field.
confidence_basis: The numeric result was produced by the pinned Blender 5.0.1 mathutils implementation and retained with exact measurements.
confounders: The harness did not evaluate a shader node graph.
Synthetic vectors do not cover importer or mesh-tangent defects.
Visual carbon-fibre behavior was not rendered.
